title: [CI:MACHINE] DO NOT MERGE: TESTING MAC FLAKE

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [jnovy](https://github.com/jnovy) for approval. Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ya, just need to run a bunch more times to see if the flake is fixed.
Sad, just to confirm can you pick https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23162 into here so we can see that it is indeed hanging at the same step.

> Sad, just to confirm can you pick https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23162 into here so we can see that it is indeed hanging at the same step.

Sorry our posts crossed.  Sure, one sec.
If there is way for direct access even better, I can will try to take a look at this tomorrow

> If there is way for direct access

Yes.  And more importantly, "MacM1-6" is isolated from the rest of the pool, so only this PR is using it.
I did some googling on "APFS" + elements from the traceback.  I'm wondering if given the number of threads involved, could this be some kind of deadlock situation?

> I did some googling on "APFS" + elements from the traceback. I'm wondering if given the number of threads involved, could this be some kind of deadlock situation?

The podman stack trace and this clearly shows us hanging in the write syscall, so if there is a deadlock it would be in the macos kernel assuming the go stack trace can be trusted.

> it would be in the macos kernel

Aye, there were a few posts I ready suspecting that.  Nothing definitive I could find though, and most references were old.  But I felt it worthwhile checking since google is often easier than low-level system debugging (esp on a closed-source OS) :stuck_out_tongue_winking_eye:
Its not a deadlock, I bumped the timeout to 10 mins and with that it seems to pass https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23162
